Targeting uses keywords and products to show your ads on search and detail pages to relevant shoppers. There are 2 targeting strategies available in Sponsored Products campaigns: automatic and manual.

The amount that you're willing to spend on this campaign each day. If the campaign spends less than your daily budget, the unspent amount can be used to increase your daily budget on the other days of the calendar month.
Ad formats are templates that help you create an ad based on what you want to promote.
Add products that you want to promote in this campaign.
Amazon optimizes your bids for placements other than top of search.
An advanced strategy that prevents your ads from displaying in specific search results or detail pages. Tip: Lower your bid for specific brands instead of excluding.
Audiences whose recent activity suggests they are likely to buy products in a certain category
Audiences whose shopping activity suggests a durable interest in specific categories
Audiences whose shopping and streaming behaviors suggest certain lifestyle preferences and affinities
Audiences with recent activity around life moments such as moving or getting married
Campaigns using "Dynamic bids - up and down" delivered 1.8x more sales, at a 4% lower ROAS compared to campaigns using "Dynamic bids - down only" (Amazon internal data, 2022).
Default bid applies to all clicks unless you set a different bid for a keyword.
Direct customers to a custom URL you’ve created (pre-order or product launches only).
Drive traffic to a Store, including subpages.

Dynamic segments are collections of products for targeting. The individual products change based on performance and relevance criteria in order to reach shoppers who are likely to engage with your ads.
Dynamic segments are collections of products to be targeted.
Each option offers a unique set of campaign configurations to optimize for your advertising goal. You won’t be able to change the goal after creating your campaign.
Engage audiences throughout the shopping and entertainment journey, from building awareness to loyalty.
Help shoppers find your product by choosing categories, products, brands, or features related to your product.
Highlight the unique benefits of the products in your ad. Use a conversational tone that matches your brand.
Impression rank is where your IS stands relative to other advertisers’ share of impressions for the same term.
Impression share (IS) is the percentage of impressions you’re getting on this term relative to all the impressions it is generating.
Keyword targeting allows you to choose keywords to show your products in customer searches and detail pages.
Match types allow you to fine-tune which customer search terms trigger your ads.
Negative keyword match types allow you to fine-tune which customer search terms trigger your ads.
Negative keywords prevent your ads from displaying when a shopper's search terms match your negative keywords. You can exclude irrelevant searches, reducing your advertising cost.
Negative product targeting prevents your ads from displaying when a shopper's search matches your negative product selections. This helps exclude irrelevant searches, reducing your advertising cost.
Orders: Number of orders the keyword has historically generated, Clicks: Number of clicks the keyword has historically generated
Pick products to advertise and we’ll create a landing page for you.
Product targeting allows you to choose specific products, categories, brands, or other product features that are relevant to the product in your ad.
Promote multiple products from a landing page of your choice.
Promote your brand using video. Drive traffic to your store detail page.
Promote your product using video. Drive traffic to your product detail page.
Reach audiences who have purchased products matching criteria you choose.
Reach audiences who have viewed or are browsing products matching criteria you choose.
Reach relevant audiences throughout their shopping and entertainment journey on or off Amazon.
Reach relevant audiences using our exclusive, prebuilt segments: Lifestyle, Interests, Life events, and In-market.
Reach shoppers who viewed detail pages in various categories, which you can refine by brand, price range, and more.
Show ads to audiences who are currently visiting products and categories that you select and similar products.
Suggested bids are calculated daily based on past bidding activity for ads similar to yours to predict bids that are more likely to win.
Suggested bids are customized to help you get impressions for your keywords.
Suggested categories are ranked by their probability of driving higher click-through rate (CTR) based on the advertised products.
Suggested keywords are based on the products in your ads. You can add the keywords that you want, and edit keywords and bids after you add them.
Suggested products based on products in your ad
Suggested products based on products in your ad.
Target individual products that are similar to products in your ad.
Targeting groups use multiple strategies to match your ads to shoppers looking for your brands, products, or categories. They can help simplify and expand your targeting.
Targeting groups use multiple strategies to match your ads to shoppers looking for your products.
Targeting uses audiences and products to help your ads appear on and off Amazon.
Targeting uses keywords and products to help your ads appear in search and detail pages.
Targeting uses keywords and products to help your ads appear in search pages.
Targeting uses product and categories to help your ads appear throughout the shopping and entertainment journey.
The amount that you're willing to spend on this campaign each day.
The landing page is where shoppers are directed after they interact with your ad.
Use your registered brand name to help shoppers identify and connect with your brand.
We’ll raise your bids (by a maximum of 100%) in real time when your ad may be more likely to convert to a sale, and lower your bids when less likely to convert to a sale.

When a customer interacts with your ad, they’ll be directed to this product's detail page or add the product to their cart. The ways customers can interact with your ad will vary depending on where they see it.
You can choose different bids for groups of search terms and products matched with the product in your ad.
You can select which products to advertise in the Creative step. At least 1 product on your landing page must remain in stock, or your campaign will be paused.
Your ads will be shown to shoppers who previously viewed the detail pages of products that are similar to your advertised products
Your ads will be shown to shoppers who previously viewed the detail pages of your advertised products
Your keywords (word combinations and phrases) are used to match your ads with search terms shoppers are using to find products.

Categories are products that are grouped by similarities, like women's running shoes.

Daily: The amount that you're willing to spend on this campaign each day. If the campaign spends less than your daily budget, the unspent amount can be used to increase your daily budget on the other days of the calendar month.

Lifetime: The total amount that you are willing to spend on this campaign.

Match types allow you to fine-tune which customer searches trigger your ads.
Broad: Contains all the keywords in any order and includes plurals, variations and related keywords.
Phrase: Contains the exact phrase or sequence of keywords.
Exact: Exactly matches the keyword or sequence of keywords

Negative keywords/product targeting prevent your ads from displaying when a shopper's search terms match your negative keywords/product selections. You can exclude irrelevant searches, reducing your advertising cost.

Orders: Number of orders the keyword has historically generated
Clicks: Number of clicks the keyword has historically generated
Suggested categories are based on the products in your ads. You can add categories and individual products that you want, and edit products, categories, and bids after they're added.
The number of times ads were displayed
The number of times your ads were clicked.
Once identified, it may take up to 3 days to remove invalid clicks.
The ratio of how often shoppers click on your ad when displayed.
Once identified, it may take up to 3 days to remove invalid clicks from your spend statistics. Date ranges that include spend from the last 3 days may be adjusted due to click and spend invalidation.
The total click or impression charges for a campaign.
This is the average amount you paid for each click on an ad.
Viewable Cost Per Mille (vCPM) = (Spend / View Impression) * 1000
The average amount you paid for every 1,000 viewable impressions. This metric only applies to campaigns with a cost type of VCPM.
Only relevant for SB, SD and STV
Total number of unique users exposed to an ad over the lifetime of the campaign or the past six months, whichever is shorter. This metric is updated daily.
Average number of times unique users were exposed to an ad over the lifetime of the campaign.
Number of detail page views for all products attributed to your ads. Attribution depends on campaign type.
Sales is the total value of products sold to shoppers attributed to your ads. Attribution depends on campaign type.
Sponsored Products: Sales from advertised products as well as other products within your brand sold by Amazon within 14 days. This doesn’t include third-party sellers.
Sponsored Brands: Sales from advertised products sold by Amazon within 14 days. Where eligible, this also includes sales from all products within your brand sold by Amazon and third-party sellers.
Sponsored Display: Sales from advertised products and all products within your brand sold by Amazon within 14 days attributed to clicks on your ad.

It can take up to 12 hours for your sales data to update. As a result, sales data may be delayed in the Today date range. We recommend waiting until all sales data is populated before evaluating campaign performance.
Payment failures and orders that are cancelled within 72 hours will be removed from sales totals.
Orders is the number of Amazon orders placed by shoppers attributed to your ads. Attribution depends on campaign type.
Sponsored Products: Orders from advertised products as well as other products within your brand sold by Amazon within 14 days. This doesn’t include third-party sellers.
Sponsored Brands: Orders from advertised products sold by Amazon within 14 days. Where eligible, this also includes orders from all products within your brand sold by Amazon and third-party sellers.
Sponsored Display: Orders from advertised products and all products within your brand sold by Amazon within 14 days of clicks on your ad.

It can take up to 12 hours for your orders data to update. As a result, orders data may be delayed in the Today date range. We recommend waiting until all orders data is populated before evaluating campaign performance.
Payment failures and orders that are cancelled within 72 hours will be removed from orders totals.
The total number of your products sold to shoppers attributed to your ads. Attribution varies by campaign type.
Sales of the purchased ASIN/SKU was different from the ASIN/SKU advertised.
Average Selling Price (ASP) = Sales / Sale Units
Only relevant for SD and STV
Return on Advertising Spend (ROAS) = Sales / Spend
The number of first-time orders for products within the brand over a 1-year period.
Only relevant for SB and SD

According to the official announcement, keep the migration timeline:
Begin with a small test batch and review spend and delivery; once results look healthy, scale to a larger batch or full-profile migration for minimal risk.
Campaigns paused for months and confirmed inactive should be archived rather than migrated—this keeps accounts tidy and avoids unnecessary approval cycles.
Do not edit ad groups, ads, keywords, or targets while migration is in progress—changes can cause failures.
The new campaign name = original + “-migrated”; start date = today.
Default objective is Drive Page Visits (CPC bidding). Create a new campaign if you need a different objective.
Migration completes first, then Amazon may take up to 72 hours to re-approve the new campaign.

SQP Analysis is a unified analytics feature that combines two Amazon Brand Analytics reports: Search Catalog Performance (SCP) and Search Query Performance (SQP).
Search Catalog Performance: Shows how each ASIN in your catalog performs in Amazon search, including impressions, clicks, add-to-carts, and purchases.
Search Query Performance: Reveals how customers interact with your products through search queries, including query volume, impressions, clicks, add-to-carts, and purchases.
Analyze product search performance: Identify ASINs with high impressions but low conversions, and take action to improve them.
Discover high-value search terms: Find keywords that drive traffic and sales to enhance your ad targeting and listing content.
Map the search-to-conversion journey: Understand which queries lead to which products, and whether they convert.
